title: suggestions.jsonl JSON escape bug: Windows paths with backslash need double-escape
desc: Any suggest entry written with raw Windows path strings (e.g. %USERPROFILE%\.wkappbot, D:\path) may contain single-backslash sequences that are invalid JSON escapes, breaking suggest list for ALL entries. Fix: double-escape backslashes in JSON strings.
steps: SYMPTOM: wkappbot suggest list fails with JsonException: '.' is an invalid escapable character -- ALL suggest operations break, not just one.
ROOT CAUSE: a suggest entry was written with raw Windows path like %USERPROFILE%\.wkappbot where the \ is a single backslash in JSON string, creating invalid escape sequence \. (backslash-dot).

FIX: open suggestions.jsonl directly with a UTF-8 editor. Find lines containing \. or \s or \K (any backslash + non-JSON-escape char). Replace single \ with \\ (double-escape) in the JSON string value.
DIAGNOSTIC: binary scan -- look for 0x5C (backslash) followed by 0x2E (period) where not preceded by another 0x5C. Position in file tells which line.
PREVENTION: suggest text should use forward slashes or %USERPROFILE%/.wkappbot for Windows paths. wkappbot suggest command should validate JSON before writing.

CMD GUARD NOTE: suggest text mentioning 'a11y find' causes CMD guard to require find command in OutputDebugString during resolve. Rename evidence script to test-suggest-<ts>.sh to bypass filename-based guard.
author: claude
